settings.title: Social networks
settings.description: Connect LinkedIn, Instagram and X to manage your content from Dome. Create a developer app on each platform and paste its credentials here — nothing is read from environment variables.
settings.no_encryption: OS keychain encryption is unavailable — secrets will be stored obfuscated, not encrypted.
settings.oauth_port_hint: Local port used to receive the OAuth redirect (http://localhost:PORT/callback/...). Change it only if the port is taken.
settings.token_placeholder: Paste an access token generated in the developer portal
settings.hint_linkedin: LinkedIn: enable "Sign In with LinkedIn using OpenID Connect" and "Share on LinkedIn" in your app. For company pages, also enable "Company pages" below and add the "Community Management API" product. Register the redirect URI above. Tokens last ~60 days.
settings.linkedin_org_hint: Manage LinkedIn company pages you administer (not just your personal profile). Requires the "Community Management API" product on your developer app. After enabling, save and reconnect so the token includes organization scopes.
settings.hint_instagram: Instagram: requires a Business/Creator account and a Meta app with the Instagram product. Easiest path: generate an access token in the Meta dashboard (API setup with Instagram login) and paste it here. Publishing needs public https media URLs.
settings.hint_x: X: create a project + app with OAuth 2.0 ('Native App' works without secret thanks to PKCE), enable Read and write, and register the redirect URI above. Note: since Feb 2026 new developers pay per use (~$0.015/post).

hub.title: Social
hub.upcoming_empty: Nothing scheduled yet. Draft a post and pick a date — Dome publishes it automatically.
hub.top_posts_empty: No metrics yet. Publish posts and refresh metrics to see what performs best.
hub.posts_empty: No posts here yet. Create your first one — you can also ask Many to draft content for you.
hub.analytics_empty: No published posts with metrics yet.
hub.growth_empty: No connected accounts yet. Connect one in Settings → Social to start tracking growth.
hub.growth_unavailable_linkedin_member: LinkedIn does not expose follower counts for personal profiles on the standard API tier. Enable company pages in Settings to track page followers.
hub.growth_pending: No snapshots yet — metrics are captured automatically every few hours, or press "Refresh metrics".

composer.title: New post
composer.body_placeholder: What do you want to share?
composer.media_placeholder: Public https image/video URL (the only option Instagram accepts)
composer.schedule_label: Schedule (leave empty to save as draft)
composer.error_empty: Write some text or add media.
composer.error_instagram_media: Instagram needs at least one media item via public https URL (photo or video).
composer.error_too_long: Text exceeds the {{limit}} character limit of the selected network.
composer.library_empty: No images or videos in this project yet.
composer.warning_instagram_local_image: Instagram cannot receive local files (its API only downloads from public URLs). Add a public URL for the Instagram variant — local files still work for LinkedIn and X.
composer.ai_need_text: Write some text first to use the AI assistant.
composer.ai_need_brief: Add topics, a campaign or an initial idea to generate a draft.
composer.ai_error: The AI could not complete the action: {{error}}
composer.schedule_calendar_note: This post will automatically show up in your Calendar.

linkedin.intro: Publish on your LinkedIn profile or company pages with your own developer app. About 5 minutes, all free.

requirements.title: Before you start
bullets: A LinkedIn account
Access to LinkedIn Developers (same login, free)
A LinkedIn company page to attach the app to — you can create a basic one in a minute if you have none

create_app.title: Create the app
create_app.body: Open the creation form with the button below and fill in:
bullets: App name: anything, e.g. 'Dome'
LinkedIn Page: pick your page (a link on the form lets you create one)
App logo: any image
Accept the terms and press 'Create app'

configure.title: Enable products and register the redirect
configure.body: Inside your app in the developer portal:
bullets: 'Products' tab → request 'Sign In with LinkedIn using OpenID Connect' AND 'Share on LinkedIn' — both approve instantly
For company pages: also request 'Community Management API' (may need LinkedIn review)
In Dome Settings → Social → LinkedIn, enable 'Company pages' before connecting
'Auth' tab → 'OAuth 2.0 settings' → 'Authorized redirect URLs' → add the URI below exactly as shown
notes: LinkedIn accepts http://localhost redirect URLs, so this desktop flow needs no https.

credentials.title: Copy Client ID and Secret
credentials.body: In the 'Auth' tab copy the Client ID and the Primary Client Secret, paste them here and press Save.
notes: The secret is stored encrypted with your OS keychain.

connect.title: Connect your account
connect.body: Press connect: your browser opens LinkedIn's consent screen. Accept and come back to Dome — the connection completes automatically.
notes: Personal tokens last ~60 days and can't auto-renew on the standard tier — when it expires, just reconnect here.
Personal-profile analytics are limited to likes and comments. Company pages can track follower counts.
After connecting with company pages enabled, Dome discovers each page you administer as a separate account.

instagram.intro: Needs a professional Instagram account and a Meta app. The dashboard token generator is the recommended path — no redirects involved.

requirements.title: Before you start
bullets: Instagram Business or Creator account — switch in the Instagram app: Settings → Account type
A Meta developer account at developers.facebook.com (free, verified Facebook account)

create_app.title: Create the Meta app
create_app.body: Open the creation wizard with the button below:
bullets: Use case: 'Other'
App type: 'Business'
Finish the wizard to reach the app dashboard

configure.title: Add the Instagram product and link your account
bullets: 'Add product' → set up 'Instagram'
Open 'API setup with Instagram business login'
Under 'Generate access tokens' add your professional Instagram account and log in with it
notes: The redirect URI below is only needed for the optional OAuth path ('Business login settings' → 'OAuth redirect URIs'). Meta may require https there — if it rejects localhost, simply use the token path in the next step.

credentials.title: Choose your path: token (recommended) or OAuth
bullets: A — recommended: in 'Generate access tokens' press 'Generate token' next to your account and copy it. You can skip the fields below and paste it in the Connect step.
B — OAuth: copy the 'Instagram app ID' and 'Instagram app secret' shown in 'API setup…', paste them here and save.

connect.title: Connect your account
connect.body: Paste the generated token below and press Connect — or use the OAuth button if you configured redirect + credentials.
notes: Instagram cannot publish text-only posts: each post needs at least one image/video hosted on a PUBLIC https URL.
Tokens are long-lived (~60 days); Dome auto-refreshes them while in use.

x.intro: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE on the X API v2. Heads-up: X moved new developers to pay-per-use pricing.

requirements.title: Before you start
bullets: An X account
A developer account at developer.x.com
notes: Since February 2026 X has no free tier for NEW developers: posting is pay-per-use (~$0.015 per post; posts containing links cost more). Older accounts may keep their previous tier.

create_app.title: Create project + app
bullets: First time: the onboarding wizard creates your Project + App automatically
Otherwise: 'Projects & Apps' in the sidebar → create a Project and an App inside it

configure.title: User authentication settings
bullets: 'User authentication settings' → 'Set up'
App permissions: 'Read and write'
Type of App: 'Native App' (public client — PKCE, no secret needed)
Callback URI / Redirect URL: add the URI below exactly
Website URL: any page of yours
notes: X accepts http://localhost callback URIs (the official xurl CLI uses one). Register it exactly as shown.

credentials.title: Copy the Client ID
credentials.body: After saving the auth settings the portal shows your OAuth 2.0 Client ID (also under 'Keys and tokens'). Paste it here and save.
notes: Client Secret is only needed if you chose a confidential 'Web App' client instead of Native App.

connect.title: Connect your account
connect.body: Press connect and authorize in the browser. Dome keeps the session alive with the refresh token.
notes: Reading tweet metrics is billed separately by X — Dome tolerates metric failures without breaking.

preview.title: Preview
preview.hint_reel_needs_video: A Reel needs a video — add one (public URL for Instagram).
preview.hint_article_needs_link: The article card appears when you add a link URL.
preview.hint_carousel_needs_images: Add at least 2 images to build a carousel.
preview.hint_needs_media: Add media to fill this format.

reports.auto_hint_on: Dome will refresh metrics and generate a new AI report on this cadence.
reports.auto_hint_off: Enable a cadence and the AI will periodically analyze your accounts and tell you how to keep focusing your content.
reports.generating_hint: The AI is analyzing your growth and post performance. This can take up to a minute.
reports.empty_hint: Generate your first AI report: growth per network, what worked, what to drop, and concrete content ideas for the next period.
